response.justification: This candidate is a talented full-stack software engineer with strong DevOps and infrastructure expertise, but is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. The position demands 5–10 years of professional C++ development on Windows platforms; Szabo has only ~6 months of documented C++ work (2014–2015) and no Windows platform experience. His career is anchored in web backends (Node.js, TypeScript), embedded systems (D language), and Linux infrastructure. Additionally, he has zero financial services or capital markets domain expertise. While his general engineering craftsmanship, CI/CD knowledge, and communication skills are strong, they cannot compensate for the absence of the mandatory technical stack.
strengths: Proven CI/CD and DevOps expertise—has built and maintained GitLab instances, CI servers, Docker infrastructure, and deployment pipelines across multiple OS platforms
Strong communication and mentoring track record—authored workshops, gave conference talks, maintains open-source projects, and demonstrates ability to distill complex concepts
Broad software engineering foundation across web, backend, and systems domains with consistent focus on software craftsmanship, testing, and clean code practices
concerns: Minimal C++ experience—only ~6 months documented at National Instruments (2014–2015), well below the required 5–10 years; most recent work is 10+ years old
No Windows platform expertise—entire career built on Linux, web backends, and cross-platform tools; zero mention of Windows development, Win32 APIs, or Windows-specific toolchains

No financial services domain experience—background is in web, media, conservation tech, and developer tooling; zero exposure to Fixed Income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets systems
